includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive database of contamination removal parameters, safety data, and laser cleaning specifications

contaminant.name: Fuel System Varnish
contaminant.description: Fuel-varnish forms as sticky polymerized deposits from degraded fuel in engines, so it builds up during storage and operation. This contamination shows unique gummy texture and dark color, distinguishing it from dry rust or oily residues because varnish resists solvents yet cracks under heat. On steel and aluminum surfaces, it adheres tightly and causes corrosion over time, while brass experiences reduced conductivity from the layer, so removal challenges include avoiding substrate damage during scraping. Laser cleaning works effectively because pulses vaporize the varnish selectively without harming metal bases.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Flammable residues or combustible dust with significant fire/explosion potential
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Fire extinguisher within 10m, avoid enclosed spaces, monitor for hot spots, spark-resistant tools

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Formaldehyde, Acrolein, Benzene - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Formaldehyde, Benzene - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Enhanced ventilation required due to fire/explosion risk - 12 ACH with carbon
